name: Connect Community Bank
description_short: Mutual bank offering real estate loans in Pacific County and surrounding areas for nearly 100 years. Member-owned, FDIC-insured, and committed to keeping mortgages local.
description_long:
Connect Community Bank has served residents of Pacific County and surrounding communities for almost 100 years as a mutual financial institution. Founded on the principle of connecting community, bank, and member resources, the bank operates with a mission to improve quality of living and support families in its service areas. The bank is FDIC-insured and maintains a routing number (325170864) for secure banking operations.

Connect Community Bank specializes in real estate financing for home purchases, construction, and property rehabilitation. They offer online mortgage applications and positioning themselves as a local alternative to national lenders. The bank emphasizes expert guidance and personalized service throughout the mortgage process, serving as a primary resource for homeownership in their community.

What distinguishes Connect Community Bank is its mutual ownership structure—one of fewer than 250 mutual banks remaining in the United States. As a mutual institution, members (depositors) are considered owners rather than customers of shareholders. This structure means the bank does not make decisions based on shareholder interests or Wall Street trends. The bank also announced a 2026 merger partnership with Oregon Coast Bank to strengthen its position. Unlike most credit unions, they pay local, state, and federal taxes, and unlike most banks, they are not controlled by external investors or stockholders.

Connect Community Bank is a legitimate, federally-insured financial institution appropriate for borrowers seeking community-focused mortgage lending. However, their service area is geographically limited to Pacific County and surrounding regions, making them inaccessible to borrowers outside this area. Prospective applicants should verify service area eligibility before applying.
services: Online banking access with username/password login
pros: Member-owned mutual bank structure prioritizes member value over shareholder profits
Nearly 100 years of history serving Pacific County and surrounding communities
FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
One of fewer than 250 mutual banks remaining in the United States
Pays local, state, and federal taxes unlike many credit unions
Online mortgage application and enrollment available
Committed to keeping mortgages local rather than selling to secondary markets
cons: Geographic service area limited to Pacific County and surrounding counties only
No specific information provided about mortgage rates, terms, or loan products
Website lacks details on FHA, VA, or other specialized mortgage programs
No information about refinancing options or current promotional rates
Upcoming 2026 merger with Oregon Coast Bank creates uncertainty about future operations
best_for: Pacific County and surrounding area residents seeking a community-focused mortgage lender
Borrowers who prioritize local lending and community involvement over national institutions
Homebuyers interested in supporting a mutual bank structure that doesn't prioritize shareholder returns
diagnosis: Connect Community Bank is best for homebuyers in Pacific County and surrounding communities who value local, member-owned banking and want to keep their mortgage away from Wall Street investors. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—this lender is not available outside their regional service area, and prospective borrowers must verify eligibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
